title: quercus × wagneri
intro: Quercus × wagneri is a hybrid plant, known as the red oak, created by crossing the white oak (Quercus alba) and the laurel oak (Quercus laurifolia). The bark and leaves of the Quercus × wagneri plant have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus × wagneri are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be taken orally to harness its potential health benefits.
